In this quick video what I want to do before we talk about scaling is I want to address what to do if none of them Worked okay, so step number one is make sure you've got enough data So if I look over here, all right, I've got 200 like specifically for all of these campaigns combined I've driven 286 visitors. So the first thing we want to look at is we want to say, okay, is it my web page All right, so I'm you know, I've got 65 conversions 286 visitors if I do the math there, which I'm gonna do here on my phone So I'm off camera. Sorry. Well, it's not just fun to watch someone do math on camera anyway But 65 divided by 286 that means my my homepage or my opt-in page there. It's converting at 22% That's fair. All right, but if this was like 35 that would mean that my page was converting in 11% I would know all right if I want to cut my my Ad cost in half. I need to fix my page. So that's like step number one Make sure That it's not your page Okay, a lot of times it's never the ads fault. It's like what happens when they click on the dang ad All right, so that's thing number one thing number two is let's say you are getting a pretty good conversion rate So and like this is you know, let's just assume nothing worked, right? So the next thing you would look at is okay Well is my cost per click overall very high in this case You know, this is actually higher than I would like it to be but we're going after Extremely expensive traffic. I mean is US only top 40% income bracket. So yeah, I'm gonna be you know paying a pretty penny per click here So overall in this case, I'm okay with this amount per click, but let's say I wasn't let's say, okay This this is way too much cost per click. What could possibly be going on in this case? We would look at what's the click-through rate. All right, so if you end up where you're noticing That I look at this one this cost per click is $6.48 and my click-through rate is half of What this one is? All right, so it's like well no wonder, okay? Now incidentally You would be like, well, how come you're not paying double per click if this is you know This is getting twice as many clicks and the reason why is YouTube is charging me more To show this ad per 1,000 people. Okay, but let's say you went and you noticed wait a gosh dang minute All right My click-through rate is terrible and you notice this across the board for everything in that case now, you know It's your ad Right if you end up where you're in a situation where your click-through rate Let's so let's say like your quick through rate is good Across the board for everything like this is obnoxious Right and yours then therefore, you know your cost per click is also okay and your conversions Generally speaking or okay, then you know all right. Well the ad is doing its job. It's getting the clicks The page is doing its job. It's getting the conversions The audience isn't buying I have an audience issue So that's how you diagnose these things right but chances are if you do a bunch of different audience combinations If you follow this exactly like we've set it up You're gonna be in a situation just like the one we're in now way, which is what we predicted Which is most stuff worked or rather most stuff didn't work and some stuff did you know, so we turned off A lot of these dang things All right, I don't know why it's not showing me the ones that I had turned off Maybe I forgot to hit pause on these so let me let me pause these are the ones I meant to turn off over here I just didn't turn them off So yeah, like most of these things worked or most of these things didn't work And I got one two three that did and that's completely normal and these guys didn't spend any money at all All right, so like there we go and this one hasn't really spent any money either. It's like no I'm not gonna spend anything for you. So that's fairly normal. So chances are this is the same experience You'll have it only takes a couple of good ones by the way And you're really you're really off to the races once again. I need to hit pause on these dang things I forgot in the last video there. So now I'm pausing these bad ones, but that's really how you look at these You know, that's the decision tree Number one is it my landing page number two Is it my cost per click if so is it my click-through rate? Is it low? If the answer to all of those is like everything's looking good, then that means it's your audience Alright, so that's how you sort of run through that Diagnostically, okay now let's talk about in the next video actually scaling up these winners Okay, because that's where we want to put our focus is on the stuff that that's working
